title: Fix updateStatus to preserve original reconcile error
description:
# GitHub Issue #186: Fix updateStatus to preserve the original reconcile error

**Category**: refactoring | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: In keystone_controller.go:139-145, when r.Status().Update() fails, the original reconcileErr is discarded and only the status update error is returned. Refactor updateStatus to use errors.Join or a multi-error pattern that preserves both the original reconcile error and the status update error, ensuring the root cause is always visible in controller logs.

**Motivation**: When a reconcile fails AND the subsequent status update also fails, the root cause of the original failure is silently lost. This makes debugging production issues significantly harder because the logs only show 'failed to update status' without any indication of what actually went wrong.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Both the original reconcile error and the status update error are returned when both fail
- [ ] Controller logs contain the root cause error, not just the status update failure
- [ ] Unit test verifies error preservation in the dual-failure case
- [ ] Single-failure cases (reconcile fails, status succeeds and vice versa) still work correctly

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/186

stories.title: Operator sees root cause error when both reconcile and status update fail
stories.want: the controller error to contain BOTH the original reconcile failure AND the status update failure
stories.so_that: I can identify the root cause of a production issue without needing to correlate separate log entries or guess what went wrong before the status update failed
criteria: When reconcile returns an error AND r.Status().Update() also fails, the returned error contains both error messages
errors.Is and errors.As can unwrap both the reconcile error and the status update error from the joined result
Controller-runtime logs the joined error, making the root cause visible in structured log output
The reconcile error appears first in the joined error string for readability

stories.title: Operator sees correct single error in single-failure cases
stories.want: the existing single-failure behavior to remain unchanged
stories.so_that: the refactoring does not regress normal error reporting when only one of the two operations fails
criteria: When reconcile fails but status update succeeds, only the reconcile error is returned (existing behavior preserved)
When reconcile succeeds but status update fails, only the status update error is returned (wrapped with 'updating status:')
When both succeed, nil is returned (existing behavior preserved)
The ctrl.Result value from the reconcile is passed through correctly in all cases

stories.title: Developer understands the error preservation pattern from documentation
stories.want: the reference documentation for updateStatus to accurately describe the errors.Join behavior
stories.so_that: I understand how errors propagate when adding new sub-reconcilers or debugging controller behavior
criteria: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md 'Status Update Pattern' section describes the errors.Join behavior
Documentation explains that both errors are preserved in the dual-failure case
Documentation references CC-0068 as the feature that introduced the change

requirements.description: updateStatus SHALL return a joined error containing both reconcileErr and the status update error when both are non-nil
requirements.rationale: The root cause error must never be silently discarded — it is critical for debugging production incidents where both reconcile and status update fail

scenarios.name: Both errors joined
scenarios.when: reconcileErr is non-nil AND r.Status().Update() returns a non-nil error
scenarios.then: the returned error contains both the reconcileErr message and the status update error message
and_then: errors.Is can match the status update error
the error string contains both messages separated by a newline

scenarios.name: Joined error is unwrappable
scenarios.when: the joined error is inspected with errors.Unwrap()
scenarios.then: both constituent errors are accessible via the standard errors unwrap interface
and_then: errors.As can extract typed errors from either constituent

requirements.description: updateStatus SHALL return only reconcileErr when the status update succeeds
requirements.rationale: Preserves existing behavior — when status update works, the original reconcile error (or nil) is the sole return

scenarios.name: Reconcile error passed through on status success
scenarios.when: reconcileErr is non-nil AND r.Status().Update() succeeds
scenarios.then: the returned error is exactly reconcileErr (unchanged)
and_then: the returned ctrl.Result matches the input result

scenarios.name: Nil error on full success
scenarios.when: reconcileErr is nil AND r.Status().Update() succeeds
and_then: the returned ctrl.Result matches the input result

requirements.description: updateStatus SHALL return only the status update error (wrapped) when reconcileErr is nil and status update fails
requirements.rationale: errors.Join(nil, err) returns just err — the behavior should match: a wrapped status update error with no spurious nil in the chain

scenarios.name: Status-only failure returns wrapped status error
scenarios.when: reconcileErr is nil AND r.Status().Update() returns a non-nil error
scenarios.then: the returned error wraps only the status update error with prefix 'updating status:'
and_then: the error string does not contain '<nil>' or empty segments

requirements.description: updateStatus SHALL pass through the ctrl.Result unchanged in all error scenarios
requirements.rationale: The result (RequeueAfter, Requeue) drives controller-runtime scheduling and must not be altered by error handling changes

scenarios.name: Result preserved on dual failure
scenarios.when: both reconcileErr and status update error are non-nil
scenarios.then: the returned ctrl.Result is ctrl.Result{} (empty), matching current behavior

scenarios.name: Result preserved on success
scenarios.when: both operations succeed AND input result has RequeueAfter set
scenarios.then: the returned ctrl.Result matches the input result exactly

requirements.description: The reference documentation SHALL describe the updated error preservation behavior
requirements.rationale: The current docs explicitly state 'status update error takes precedence' which will become incorrect after the fix

scenarios.name: Documentation reflects errors.Join
scenarios.when: a developer reads docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md 'Status Update Pattern' section
scenarios.then: the section describes that both errors are joined via errors.Join when both are non-nil

tasks.title: Refactor updateStatus to use errors.Join and add unit tests for all error combinations (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:139-145, replace the current updateStatus implementation that discards reconcileErr on status update failure. Add 'errors' to imports. Change the error branch from `return ctrl.Result{}, fmt.Errorf("updating status: %w", err)` to `return ctrl.Result{}, errors.Join(reconcileErr, fmt.Errorf("updating status: %w", err))`. Add 8 unit tests to keystone_controller_test.go: (1) TestUpdateStatus_BothErrors_Joined — use interceptor.Funcs{SubResourceUpdate: ...} to fail status update, pass a non-nil reconcileErr, assert returned error contains both messages. (2) TestUpdateStatus_JoinedError_IsUnwrappable — verify errors.Unwrap on the joined error. (3) TestUpdateStatus_ReconcileErrorOnly — status update succeeds, assert returned error equals reconcileErr. (4) TestUpdateStatus_NoErrors — both succeed, assert nil. (5) TestUpdateStatus_StatusErrorOnly — reconcileErr is nil, status update fails, assert returned error wraps only the status error. (6) TestUpdateStatus_StatusErrorOnly_NoNilSegments — verify error string has no '<nil>' segments. (7) TestUpdateStatus_ResultPassthrough_DualFailure — verify ctrl.Result{} on dual failure. (8) TestUpdateStatus_ResultPassthrough_WithRequeueAfter — verify RequeueAfter preserved on success. Follow existing interceptor pattern from reconcile_hpa_test.go.

tasks.title: Update keystone-reconciler reference doc to describe error joining behavior (REQ-005)
tasks.description: In docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md, update the 'Status Update Pattern' section (lines 212-217). Replace the sentence 'If the status update itself fails, the status update error takes precedence and is returned to controller-runtime for exponential backoff.' with a description of the new errors.Join behavior: when the status update fails and a reconcile error is also present, both errors are joined via errors.Join so the root cause is never lost. Add CC-0068 to the feature list in the frontmatter (line 4). Keep the rest of the section unchanged.

test_specifications.story: Operator sees root cause error when both reconcile and status update fail
test_specifications.expected: When reconcileErr is non-nil and Status().Update() fails, returned error contains both error messages and both are unwrappable [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator sees root cause error when both reconcile and status update fail
test_specifications.expected: The joined error supports errors.Unwrap returning both constituent errors [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator sees correct single error in single-failure cases
test_specifications.expected: When reconcileErr is non-nil but Status().Update() succeeds, returned error equals reconcileErr exactly [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator sees correct single error in single-failure cases
test_specifications.expected: When reconcileErr is nil and Status().Update() succeeds, returned error is nil [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator sees correct single error in single-failure cases
test_specifications.expected: When reconcileErr is nil and Status().Update() fails, returned error wraps only the status error with 'updating status:' prefix [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator sees correct single error in single-failure cases
test_specifications.expected: When reconcileErr is nil and Status().Update() fails, the error string does not contain '<nil>' or empty segments from errors.Join [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator sees correct single error in single-failure cases
test_specifications.expected: When both errors are non-nil, the returned ctrl.Result is ctrl.Result{} (empty) [unit]

test_specifications.story: Operator sees correct single error in single-failure cases
test_specifications.expected: When status update succeeds and input result has RequeueAfter set, the returned ctrl.Result preserves RequeueAfter [unit]

review_criteria: updateStatus returns errors.Join(reconcileErr, statusErr) when both are non-nil — verified by TestUpdateStatus_BothErrors_Joined
Existing single-failure behavior is preserved — verified by TestUpdateStatus_ReconcileErrorOnly and TestUpdateStatus_StatusErrorOnly
ctrl.Result is passed through unchanged in all scenarios — verified by TestUpdateStatus_ResultPassthrough
No import changes beyond adding 'errors' to keystone_controller.go
docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md Status Update Pattern section accurately describes the new behavior and references CC-0068
All existing tests in keystone_controller_test.go continue to pass without modification
implementation_notes: The fix is a 2-line change in updateStatus: (1) add 'errors' to imports, (2) replace `return ctrl.Result{}, fmt.Errorf("updating status: %w", err)` with `return ctrl.Result{}, errors.Join(reconcileErr, fmt.Errorf("updating status: %w", err))`. When reconcileErr is nil, errors.Join(nil, statusErr) returns statusErr (Go spec: nil arguments are discarded). When both are non-nil, the joined error's Error() returns both messages separated by newline. Controller-runtime logs the returned error, so both errors will appear in controller logs automatically. For tests, use interceptor.Funcs{SubResourceUpdate: func(ctx context.Context, c client.Client, subResourceName string, obj client.Object, opts ...client.SubResourceUpdateOption) error { return fmt.Errorf("simulated status update error") }} to inject status update failures. Call updateStatus directly (not via Reconcile) for focused unit tests.
